title: TradersPost bot executing multiple times?
author: 1etsplay
selftext:
So before I start I’m brand new to trading bots as well as this sub

I started a paper trading account on TradersPost because I’m pretty interested in computer science. I made the most basic one I could. It follows the super trend indicator on tradingview. I made it trade bitcoin 24/7 only in whole shares and allowing it to take bullish and bearish positions with 100% of the accounts cash.

However after the first day I started to notice trades in the logs that exceed the paper value of the account. I’m considering taking this strategy to my actual account, but since I dont want to incur a negative cash balance by accident I realized I needed to fix this.

To my best understanding, the first screenshot shows it taking exiting a bullish position and entering a bearish position. Both of these trades have a value of about $120,000 which checks out with the current value of the paper account. However after that it exits that last bearish position it buys two bitcoin three times. 

The third photo shows the trading view chart during the time period that this occurred. To my view I see nothing odd with the chart.

Finally, the second photo shows the alert log which has three buy signals in a row.

To sum up my question would both; A. Why am getting multiple signals from trading view and B. why is tradersPost allowsing these trades to go through given that they give me a negative cash balance.

P.S. The trading view alerts are only allowed to trigger once per bar.
